stories.title: Rosalía pide perdón tras la tormenta digital en Argentina por compartir un vídeo de la victoria de España
stories.summary: La cantante Rosalía se ha visto obligada a disculparse tras compartir en sus redes sociales un post que ha generado polémica en Argentina. La cantante catalana reposteó un vídeo de la antigua actriz de cine para adultos Mia Khalifa, en la que la ahora influencer celebraba el triunfo de España desde un balcón en Nueva York. De fondo en esa grabación sonaba La Perla, una de las canciones más famosas del disco LUX de Rosalía, mientras un texto sobreimpreso proclamaba que la vida sonaba distinta ahora que “las Perlas” (en referencia al equipo argentino) habían sido derrotadas.
stories.trend: que paso con rosalia y argentina

stories.title: Silvia Intxaurrondo y RTVE buscan fórmulas para que la presentadora siga en la cadena incluso si pierde su demanda
stories.summary: Silvia Intxaurrondo y los directivos de Radio Televisión Española (RTVE) buscan fórmulas para que la presentadora siga en la cadena pública incluso si pierde su demanda laboral contra la corporación. Según ha podido confirmar EL ESPAÑOL-Invertia con fuentes conocedoras, la principal alternativa podría ser que la periodista renuncie a su contrato como personal fijo de RTVE para que luego selle una nueva vinculación como personal externo. Estas mismas fuentes indican que hay una clara voluntad de las partes para que la profesional siga en la pública presentando La hora de La 1 con lo que se alejan los fantasmas de una posible salida de la cadena.

stories.title: Un automobiliste de 18 ans mis en examen à Marseille pour tentative de meurtre sur un policier après un refus d'obtempérer
stories.summary: Un homme de 18 ans a été mis en examen et placé en détention provisoire pour tentative de meurtre sur une personne dépositaire de l'autorité publique après avoir grièvement blessé un policier lors d'un refus d'obtempérer, lundi à Marseille, indique jeudi 23 juillet le procureur de la République de Marseille, Nicolas Bessone. Trois mineurs, âgés de 13 et 16 ans, qui se trouvaient à bord du véhicule volé, ont également été mis en examen pour des faits liés à cette affaire.

stories.title: Wreckage of Pan Am plane that shaped aviation safety found 74 years on
stories.summary: 21 hours ago Zahra Fatima The wreckage of a Pan American Airways plane has been found 74 years after it plunged into the Atlantic Ocean in a crash that prompted mandatory airline safety briefings. The Clipper Endeavor was found 2,000ft (610m) below sea level off the coast of Puerto Rico with a sonar-equipped drone. It went down on 11 April 1952 following multiple-engine failure shortly after take-off. Everyone onboard survived the impact - but passengers struggled to locate life vests and rafts as the plane rapidly sank. Of the 69 passengers and crew onboard, just 17 survived.

stories.title: « Ici tout commence » : Catherine Davydzenka dénonce les « contre-vérités » de Stéphane Blancafort
stories.summary: France 22/07/2026 14:42 Actualisé le 22/07/2026 14:55 Catherine Davydzenka et Stéphane Blancafort, en couple à la ville, ont eu une violente dispute qui a conduit à la mise à l’écart du comédien de la série de TF1. Arnold Jerocki / WireImage Catherine Davydzenka lors d’un photocall pour la série de TF1 « Ici tout commence » en juin 2023 à Monte-Carlo. L’affaire se poursuit.

stories.title: Meurtre de Thomas à Crépol : la circonstance aggravante de «racisme» finalement retenue par les juges
stories.summary: C’est un rebondissement majeur dans ce dossier. Les magistrats en charge de l’enquête sur le meurtre de Thomas, à Crépol, le 18 novembre 2023, ont informé les parties de la mise en examen supplétive des 14 mis en cause pour meurtre et tentative de meurtre avec la circonstance aggravante de «racisme», a appris Le Figaro de sources concordantes. La nouvelle intervient alors que l’enquête vient d’être clôturée, et qu’un procès se profilait pour les mis en examen l’année prochaine. Selon nos informations, cette étape survient après de nouveaux interrogatoires des mis en cause, qui se sont déroulés en début d’été, et qui ont abouti à cette mise en examen.

stories.title: Arsenal reach €40m Christos Tzolis agreement with Club Brugge - The Athletic
stories.summary: Arsenal have reached a €40million (£34m) agreement with Club Brugge for the signing of winger Christos Tzolis. The north London club are in the process of finalising a move for the 24-year-old. The Athletic reported on June 12 that the Premier League club were exploring a deal for the Tzolis, with sources saying that they believed a deal could be possible for around €40m. Arsenal have been looking to strengthen their attack this summer, with the pursuit of Tzolis separate from any interest in signing Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa or a move for any other target. The departure of versatile forward Leandro Trossard to Besiktas in a €20m deal was confirmed this week.

stories.title: Haaland handed a RECORD price for 2026/27 Fantasy
stories.summary: Man City star surpasses his own previous mark after ending 2025/26 as the top-scoring player Erling Haaland has become the most expensive player of all time after being priced at £15.5m for 2026/27 Fantasy Premier League . Manchester City’s Norwegian forward surpasses his own previous record of £15.0m from the 2024/25 campaign. Haaland now appears four times in the list of the nine most expensive players of all time, having cost £14.0m at the start of both 2023/24 and last season, 2025/26.

stories.title: Monaco : Aladji Bamba va signer en Angleterre pour plus de 40M€
stories.summary: Actif sur le mercato avec les arrivées d’Ansu Fati (FC Barcelone), Sadibou Sané (FC Metz) et Flavio Nazinho (Cercle Bruges), l’AS Monaco doit aussi vendre. Le club de la Principauté s’est séparé pour le moment de Caio Henrique (Ajax Amsterdam), Kassoum Ouattara (Besiktas), Radoslaw Majecki (Pafos) et Krépin Diatta (libre). Les Monégasques sont sur le point de réaliser une grosse vente supplémentaire. Selon nos informations, le club anglais de Newcastle a bouclé l’arrivée du milieu défensif Aladji Bamba (20 ans). Le joueur va arriver aujourd’hui en Angleterre dans le cadre d’un transfert s’élevant à 41 millions d’euros, dont 5 millions d’euros de bonus.

stories.title: "Jeep del rifugio devastata a colpi d'ascia: vetri infranti e gomme bucate, hanno tentato di spingerla giù dalla scarpata". Violente intimidazioni al Rifugio Gino e Massimo a pochi giorni dalla 'pastasciutta antifascista'
stories.summary: Il "RifuGino", in località Grioni sulle Orobie Valtellinesi, è stato bersaglio di una serie di pesanti vandalismi. A inizio mese, gli asini di un alpeggio vicino erano stati rinchiusi da ignoti nel bivacco del rifugio, causando danni e sporcizia alla struttura. Poi, la notte scorsa, dopo una serie di intimidazioni via social, la jeep di servizio è stata completamente distrutta. Ne parliamo con la rifugista Anna Savonitto Questo articolo si rispecchia nei nove punti del Manifesto , di cui il Comitato scientifico dell’AltraMontagna è garante. "Non siamo disposti a tollerare questi atti violenti, vili, codardi e di una bassezza umana sconcertante .

stories.title: Een bossie gladiolen: de meestgehoorde muziek tijdens de Nijmeegse Vierdaagse
stories.summary: De Nijmeegse Vierdaagse is in volle gang en heeft elk jaar zijn eigen hit. Wat zijn de grootste hits en wat wordt dit jaar uit volle borst meegezongen? In Langs de Lijn En Omstreken wordt een duik genomen in de muziekgeschiedenis van het wandelevenement. Wandelen en muziek: dat gaat hand in hand op de Vierdaagse, die jaarlijks tienduizenden wandelaars trekt. Online zijn er verschillende, indrukwekkend lange afspeellijsten te vinden. Ook zijn er speciale sites met marsmuziek voor de militaire deelnemers. Wat voor muziek klinkt er het meest op en langs de marsroute? Twee jaar geleden turfden wandelaars Nick en Dick welke vier nummers ze het meest hoorden.

stories.title: DIRECT. Incendie en Gironde : 20 000 personnes évacuées depuis mercredi soir, 2 400 hectares brûlés... Suivez les dernières infos ce jeudi...
stories.summary: DIRECT. Incendie en Gironde : 20 000 personnes évacuées depuis mercredi soir, 2 400 hectares brûlés... Suivez les dernières infos ce jeudi... - Un incendie d’une grande virulence a parcouru 3 100 hectares depuis mercredi près du bassin d’Arcachon, où près de 20 000 habitants et touristes ont été évacués au total dans ce secteur de la forêt des Landes de Gascogne, déjà ravagée par les flammes en 2022. - Environ 700 pompiers et 150 engins sont toujours engagés ce jeudi contre les flammes, appuyés dans les airs par deux Canadairs, deux Airtractors, ces petits avions bombardiers d’eau, un Dash et un hélicoptère de reconnaissance. - Aucune victime n’est recensée à ce stade par les autorités.

stories.title: Ortega Smith ocupará el despacho y el escaño de Ábalos en el Congreso tras su paso al Grupo Mixto
stories.summary: El concejal y diputado Javier Ortega Smith , quien la semana pasada anunció que dejaba el Grupo Parlamentario Vox en el Congreso, ocupará a partir de ahora el despacho del exministro José Luis Ábalos y el escaño que ostentó en la Cámara Baja durante ...

stories.title: Milk urgently recalled from UK supermarkets due to contamination fears
stories.summary: Jump to content Thank you for registering Please refresh the page or navigate to another page on the site to be automatically logged in Please refresh your browser to be logged in All News Sport Culture Lifestyle Do not drink the affected milk (Getty/iStock) An urgent "do not consume" warning has been issued for Graham's Family Dairy semi-skimmed milk due to potential contamination with penicillin. The recall specifically applies to one-litre, two-litre, and single-pint bottles of semi-skimmed milk with an expiration date of 31 July 2026, sold across the UK.

stories.title: Sgombero del Castello delle Cerimonie La Sonrisa: i mobili del ristorante-hotel finiranno in un magazzino
stories.summary: L'atto di sgombero è nero su bianco ormai da maggio, ma la giunta comunale di Sant'Antonio Abate, piccolo centro ai piedi dei Monti Lattari, in provincia di Napoli, ha dato mandato esecutivo all'ordinanza che sfratta il leggendario, "Castello delle Cerimonie" al secolo ristorante-hotel "La Sonrisa" fondato da don Antonio Polese dai terreni su cui è in attività da mezzo secolo e più. Apprende Fanpage che con una delibera di giunta approvata lunedì 20 luglio, l'amministrazione guidata dalla sindaca Ilaria Abagnale fa entrare il countdown per La Sonrisa in una fase operativa. Il provvedimento di sgombero è immediatamente eseguibile.

stories.title: More elected leaders express concerns over birthright citizenship bill
stories.summary: More of Guam’s elected leaders, including Gov. Lou Leon Guerrero, have expressed their concern regarding the proposed “End Birthright Citizenship for Territories Act”. “The consequences for real families in our villages deserve a clear-eyed look. That responsibility falls first on Capitol Hill — and especially on Congressman Griffith — for any effort to change, without our collective input, the 75-year-old federal law that officially welcomed Guam into the American family,” the Republican Party wrote in a statement Wednesday on how the bill changes the Organic Act of Guam.

stories.title: Proprietario di 1.400 immobili non dichiara 42 milioni di euro di canoni: scattano le confische, resa dei conti a Torino
stories.summary: Dopo la pena patteggiata – tre anni ai domiciliari e un accordo per versare circa sette milioni di euro evasi al fisco – adesso per i beni è il momento della resa dei conti. Nei giorni scorsi, i militari del nucleo di polizia economico-finanziaria di Torinoautoriciclaggio il provvedimento di confisca per 1,1 milioni di euro a Giorgio Molino, 83 anni, “ras delle soffitte” di Torino. Certo: per uno che come lui ha avuto disponibilità di un impero da quasi 1.500 immobili, non è che una parte del gigantesco patrimonio di cui dispone.

stories.title: Feu dans une porcherie en Sarthe : 2 200 animaux ont péri dans l’incendie
stories.summary: Un feu s’est déclaré ce mercredi 22 juillet 2026, en fin de soirée, dans une porcherie située à Sablé-sur-Sarthe. Environ 2 200 animaux ont péri dans l’incendie.Réveillé par ses chiens, le responsable de l’exploitation agricole située entre les communes de Sablé-sur-Sarthe et Saint-Brice (en Mayenne) est sorti et s’est aperçu du départ de feu. Tout est ensuite allé très vite, le feu ayant communiqué entre trois bâtiments.Le feu s’est arrêté « à 10 mètres de la maison »Le bilan est très lourd.

stories.title: Festival Kwaku stopt in huidige vorm, organisatie overlegt met gemeente over alternatief
stories.summary: Het Amsterdamse Kwaku Summer Festival stopt volgend jaar in de huidige vorm. De organisatie noemt als reden de stijgende kosten, aangescherpte vergunningsvoorwaarden van de gemeente en toegenomen organisatorische complexiteit. Voor de organisatie was het geen makkelijk besluit. "Kwaku is meer dan een festival - het is een uniek maatschappelijk fenomeen dat vanouds voor heel veel mensen veel betekent", staat in een persbericht. Het is nog niet duidelijk of het multiculturele festival helemaal verdwijnt of na de editie van deze zomer toch nog terugkeert in een andere vorm. De organisatie meldt hierover in overleg te zijn met het stadsdeel Zuidoost.

stories.title: Einspruch des TSV im Toto-Pokal: »Er war nicht spielberechtigt«
stories.summary: Die 1:3-Niederlage in der 1. Runde des Toto-Pokals des TSV Neudrossenfeld gegen Eintracht Bamberg hat ein Nachspiel. Nach dem Abpfiff hat der Bayernligist Einspruch gegen die Wertung der Partie eingelegt. Nun liegt der Ball beim Bayerischen Fußball-Verband (BFV), der am grünen Tisch über den Ausgang entscheiden muss. Was war passiert? Bis zur 71. Minute beim Stand von 1:2 lief die Begegnung in geordneten Bahnen. Dann wechselten die Bamberger erneut – und der neue Mann schlug augenblicklich ein. In der 75. Minute erzielte der Eingewechselte das vermeintliche 3:1 für den FCE. Doch die Freude währte nur kurz, denn plötzlich unterbrach das Schiedsrichtergespann die Partie.

stories.title: UniCredit steht kurz vor Commerzbank-Übernahme
stories.summary: Die italienische Großbank UniCredit steht kurz vor der Übernahme der Commerzbank. Bankchef Orcel sucht dazu jetzt das Gespräch mit allen Beteiligten. Willkommen ist er allerdings nicht. Geht es nach UniCredit-Chef Andrea Orcel, ist die Übernahme der Commerzbank bereits beschlossene Sache. "Die Commerzbank entwickelt sich von einer attraktiven Finanzbeteiligung zu einer strategischen Transaktion mit substanziellem industriellen Wert durch die Umsetzung unserer bewährten Blaupause", heißt es in einer Präsentation von Orcel zu den Quartalszahlen. Mit den Genehmigungen der Aufseher sei vielleicht noch in diesem Jahr zu rechnen.

stories.title: Maxence Lacroix to Chelsea: Crystal Palace defender is quick, excels when one-on-one and is a leader now too
stories.summary:
Chelsea have not paid a transfer fee for a player aged 26 or over since the summer of 2022 with the ill-fated acquisitions of Raheem Sterling and Kalidou Koulibaly. But Crystal Palace’s Maxence Lacroix is a player worth relaxing the policy for.

Far from a veteran, Lacroix, 26, is entering his prime after two strong seasons with Palace. Beyond Selhurst Park, perhaps the Frenchman has gone a little under the radar. But, particularly since Marc Guehi's exit, there is growing awareness of his key role.

Oliver Glasner, who worked with him in both Germany and England, rather summed it up with his own verdict on the player recently.

stories.title: 'I am still alive': Indian activist on hunger strike for 26 days loses 11kg
stories.summary: Sonam Wangchuk has been the main draw at the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protest in Delhi. The violence has stirred public anger and galvanised India's opposition, posing a challenge for Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government. Modi has not commented directly on the protests or engaged with the protesters, but on Thursday, he posted a statement on X that is being seen as an attempt to reach out to students who have been frustrated with repeated exam paper leaks . "Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth," he wrote. "We have decided to set up fast-track courts to ensure swift and stringent punishment for those involved in paper leaks...

stories.title: #RFCASSE : Les Verts repartent de Glasgow avec des enseignements positifs !
stories.summary: #RFCASSE : Les Verts repartent de Glasgow avec des enseignements positifs ! Ce mercredi, partis défier les Rangers dans leur antre de l’Ibrox Stadium, les Verts ont été battus sur le fil après une prestation cohérente (2-1). Le onze pour débuter Pour ce troisième rendez-vous estival, Ian Cathro doit toujours composer sans plusieurs éléments majeurs de son groupe comme les mondialistes, Ben Old et Augustine Boakye, ou encore les blessés, tels que Chico Lamba et Mahmoud Jaber. L’entraîneur des Verts s’appuie donc sur la même défense que face au Servette, mais fait le choix de débuter avec Thierno Ballo, en l'absence de Zuriko Davitashvili et Lucas Stassin.

stories.title: Business rate cut not enough, says Michelin-star chef
stories.summary: Alex Claridge, owner of The Wilderness in Birmingham, said the proposal did not go far enough. For Michelin-starred chef Alex Claridge, running an independent restaurant has become a difficult task. Despite the acclaim his Birmingham restaurant The Wilderness commands, Claridge says the reality behind the kitchen doors is one of soaring costs, shrinking margins and constant uncertainty. It's a picture he believes is shared by independent hospitality businesses across the country.

stories.title: Ex-Coronation Street actor to receive 'substantial' libel damages from the Sun publisher
stories.summary: Qasim Akhtar is to receive "substantial" damages to settle a High Court libel claim from a story last eyar. Former Coronation Street actor Qasim Akhtar is to receive "substantial" damages from the Sun's publisher, to settle a High Court libel claim. Akhtar who played Zeedan Nazir in the ITV soap from 2014 to 2018 and then 2021 to 2023, sued News Group Newspapers (NGN) earlier this year. A story in the Sun had falsely alleged Akhtar had "backed" and "teamed up with" Islamic cleric Uthman ibn Farooq, was associated with extremism, radicalisation and violence, and had "moved to Pakistan" and "trained with guns".

stories.title: Illegal waste investigation sees £275k forfeited
stories.summary: The investigation spans Herefordshire, Worcestershire and Shropshire, police say. Three businesses that are being investigated by police who are looking into illegal waste activity have forfeited a total of more than £275,000. West Mercia Police said additional crimes had been identified in its investigation across Herefordshire, Worcestershire and Shropshire. The force added that more assets were under review and a money laundering investigation was still active. It comes after two men were arrested at an address in Drakes Broughton, Worcestershire, in January. Six firearms were seized at that location, along with about £18,000 in cash.

stories.title: El aviso rojo por altas temperaturas suspende actividades y trastoca horarios de apertura de centros turísticos, sociales y religiosos
stories.summary: Tiempo El Ayuntamiento adopta medidas para proteger a la población y mascotas de la ola de calor El miércoles y jueves se espera sobrepasar los 40 grados en algunas pedanías del interior y en la ciudad se alcanzarán los 38 grados Publicado el 7 de julio de 2026 La AEMET ha elevado a nivel naranja el aviso por altas temperaturas, lo que aumenta el riesgo extremo de incendios forestales. Ayer por la tarde, se produjo un incendio de vegetación junto al Parque Natural de El Hondo.

stories.title: Richards hopeful of completing medal set in Glasgow
stories.summary: Double Olympic champion Matt Richards is hoping to claim Commonwealth gold and complete his major medal collection. The 23-year-old swimmer won Olympic golds in 2021 and 2024 and has multiple world and European titles to his name. But four fourth-place finishes in Birmingham in 2022 has left a hole in an otherwise glittering trophy cabinet. "It would mean the world," said Richards, of winning gold in Glasgow 2026. "Every time I've ever stood at the top of the podium I've heard the British national anthem, which is fantastic and amazing and every time it's special. "But I've never been able to stand at the top of the podium and hear the Welsh anthem and that's something important to me.

stories.title: Poorest children in England 40% more likely to be hospitalised for lung infections
stories.summary: Children living in the poorest areas across England are 40% more likely to be admitted to hospital for respiratory infections, an analysis of NHS England data shows, with experts raising the alarm on the impact of air pollution on young people’s health. The analysis, based on data obtained via freedom of information requests by the charity Impact on Urban Health, revealed 1,941 out of every 100,000 children living in the most deprived fifth of England were admitted to hospital for respiratory illnesses in 2024-25.

stories.title: England U16 player fight was resolved quickly - FA
stories.summary: The FA says a fight between two players at an England Under-16s camp was caused by an "internal matter". A fight between England Under-16 players at a camp in Turkey was caused by an "internal matter" which was "quickly resolved", says the FA. An online clip from the camp in late-2025 shows two players fighting in a hotel room, while several others film and encourage them. The FA said the issue has been settled and no further action will be taken. An FA spokesperson said: "We were made aware of an internal matter during an Under-16s camp in October 2025.

Die Senioren übergaben einem falschen Polizeibeamten das Geld an ihrer Wohnanschrift“, heißt es aus dem Präsidium.

stories.title: I wanted a clock that never needed setting. Things escalated.
stories.summary: Sure, maybe your clock has a backup battery, but mine has a deployment pipeline! Let’s be clock bros Sure, maybe your clock has a backup battery, but mine has a deployment pipeline! Rapid prototyping! Credit: Lee Hutchinson Rapid prototyping! Credit: Lee Hutchinson I wanted a clock that, annoyingly, didn’t seem to exist. Since childhood, my bedside clocks have been a series of red, seven-segment LED clock-radio specials from Walmart or Target. They are invariably cheap, simple, and long-lived—but they require manual intervention at the start and end of Daylight Saving Time and whenever the power flickers.

stories.title: Johnson-Thompson and Asher-Smith out of Commonwealths
stories.summary: Olympic silver medallist Katarina Johnson-Thompson pulls out of the Commonwealth Games because of injury. Olympic silver medallist Katarina Johnson-Thompson has pulled out of the Commonwealth Games because of injury. Dina Asher-Smith and 400m Olympic silver medalist Matt Hudson-Smith, who were both set to run relays for Team England in Glasgow, have also been replaced, as has Asher-Smith's sprint team-mate Daryll Neita and 400m runner Amber Anning. Johnson-Thompson, 33, was due to compete in her third Commonwealth Games heptathlon, having won gold on the Gold Coast in 2018 and at Birmingham in 2022.

stories.title: Gary Lineker among millionaires asking Andy Burnham to tax them more
stories.summary: A group including the former England footballer write to the new PM telling him "we can afford it". Millionaires including former footballer Gary Lineker and music producer Brian Eno have written to new prime minister Andy Burnham asking to be taxed more. In an open letter, 120 well-off Britons told Burnham: "We can afford it. We're not talking about higher taxes on those who get up and go to work for their income every day, but on the very richest whose income is derived from the wealth they hold." Organised by Patriotic Millionaires, the letter said it would lead to a more equal society, and urged a "devolution of wealth and power from the very richest".

stories.title: Scheffler not keen on McIlroy-DeChambeau feud
stories.summary: Scottie Scheffler not keen on Rory McIlroy and Bryson DeChambeau feud Updated / Thursday, 23 Jul 2026 10:38 Getty World number one Scottie Scheffler is playing in the 3M Open in Minnesota, starting today World number one Scottie Scheffler admits he has "never been a big fan of personal jabs" after Rory McIlroy publicly criticised Bryson DeChambeau at last week's Open Championship.

stories.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
stories.summary: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region. About 12,000 people have been evacuated from the south-west of France and authorities are moving 8,800 others as wildfires spread in the Gironde region, the local prefect has said. Sophie Brocas told reporters 2,400 hectares (5,900 acres) of land had already been destroyed around the Bay of Arcachon. Many of those who had to leave the area overnight were visitors to local campsites on the Lège-Cap-Ferret peninsula and the latest evacuations also involved campsites as a precaution. Wildfires have also broken out in Spain and Italy in recent days.

stories.title: LIVE: Kijk hier naar het skûtsjesilen bij Langweer
stories.summary: Kijk hier naar de vijfde wedstrijd van het SKS-kampioenschap skûtsjesilen 2026. Vandaag is de wedstrijd op de Langwarder Wielen. Kan Langweer stunten op het thuiswater? En welk skûtsje grijpt de macht in het algemeen klassement? De wedstrijd begint om 14.00 uur met een tien-minutenschot. Het startschot is om 14.10 uur. stories.title: Zimbabwe look to expose vulnerable India searching for winning formula
stories.summary: Big picture: Shreyas Iyer looking for first win India 's role in helping smaller cricket economies generate income via TV rights generally dominates chatter ahead of a Zimbabwe tour. But series defeats to Ireland and England have veered the discussion towards India's T20I failings and team combinations. When they arrived in Zimbabwe two years ago, they were led by Shubman Gill. Now, T20Is are the only format Gill isn't part of. The captaincy mantle has been passed on from Suryakumar Yadav to Shreyas Iyer , who is winless as captain after seven games.

stories.title: ‘Why do I feel crappy?’: women are learning about their luteal phases - and Maha is weaponizing it
stories.summary: E mily Rae Detrick, a health coach, wants women to be more in touch with their menstrual cycle in its entirety. This, she said on an October episode of Culture Apothecary with Alex Clark, a podcast popular among “Make America Healthy Again” (Maha) adherents, is “a way that you honor God and the way we were created”. It also serves her self-assigned mission to help “make America fertile again”. In the episode and on social media, Detrick speaks at length about the luteal phase, the 14-ish days between ovulation and menstruation when women are more likely to experience irritability, bloating and food cravings.

stories.title: The right-wing boomers protesting data centers have a lot in common with the left
stories.summary: On a gray, humid Saturday morning in central Florida, a little under a dozen people gathered outside the Spring Hill Branch Library to protest the construction of a hyperscale data center in their community. There was no immediate threat — the Hernando County commission had unanimously approved a one-year moratorium on such developments in June — but the organizers weren’t satisfied. A temporary pause wouldn’t be enough. They wanted a ban. “We are going to fight, fight, fight,” said a 71-year-old woman named Carol, who was manning a table with flyers and a petition the group, No Data Centers Hernando County, planned on presenting to the local government.

stories.title: ¿Puedo dejar la basura en el rellano? La Ley de Propiedad Horizontal lo aclara
stories.summary: La convivencia entre vecinos no siempre es sencilla. Compartir espacios comunes implica respetar unas normas básicas para garantizar el bienestar de todos los residentes, pero no siempre ocurre así. Los ruidos fuera del horario permitido, la suciedad en las zonas comunes y el uso inadecuado del ascensor o discusiones por pequeños conflictos forman parte del día a día en muchas comunidades de propietarios.

stories.title: Schipper Joure: "We hebben echt enorm staan schreeuwen naar het publiek"
stories.summary: Schipper Rinus de Jong uit Joure is bekomen van de aanvaring van gisteren. Hij belandde met zijn skûtsje in het publiek, toen het eerste schip al over de finish was gekomen in de Lange Sleat. De Jong zegt dat de SKS moet praten over het veranderen van de regels, zodat zoiets volgend jaar niet weer kan gebeuren. "Het was hartstikke spannend, maar gelukkig is het allemaal goed afgelopen. We hebben niet veel schade: een paar krassen op het zwaard en een deukje in de kop, dus dat valt reuze mee, maar het was wel levensgevaarlijk wat er gebeurde.

stories.title: Politica Daniëlle T. uit Stadskanaal veroordeeld voor stalken van radiopresentator
stories.summary: Ze hadden een affaire, radiopresentator Fernando Halman en politica Daniëlle T. uit Stadskanaal. Na die affaire, die hij beëindigde, kreeg Halman in 2023 honderden telefoontjes en berichtjes van haar. Hij deed aangifte en T. werd woensdag veroordeeld tot een taakstraf en een voorwaardelijke celstraf. De rechtbank vindt het bewezen dat de vrouw stelselmatig inbreuk heeft gemaakt op de levenssfeer van de presentator van NPO's jongerenzender FunX. Ze ziet, zo schrijft de rechtbank, zichzelf als slachtoffer en ontkent dat ze iets strafbaars gedaan heeft. Daarom legt de rechtbank haar naast een taakstraf van 120 uur en een voorwaardelijke celstraf van drie maanden ook een contactverbod op.

stories.title: White House report says Trump can usher in a "new golden age" of science
stories.summary: The resulting report is a near-random mixture of real problems and grievances. Gold-plated? It's a golden age, of sorts, in the White House at least. Credit: Bloomberg On Tuesday, the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) released a report entitled “Science: A New Golden Age,” in which it lays out how it has viewed science, found it lacking, and believes the Trump administration is in the perfect position to fix things. It’s a bit unexpected coming from an administration that has been proposing crippling funding cuts to research and trying to enable political appointees to terminate grants awarded based on scientific merit.

stories.title: Department of Justice received 78 legal aid invoices for individual’s theft charges
stories.summary: Solicitors secured 78 criminal legal aid certificates for representing one accused charged with theft offences before the District Court last year. The Dublin case, involving at least as many charges of theft, was one of more than 30 in the last 18 months to accrue dozens of legal aid certificates, which are essentially payment invoices, according to data from the Department of Justice . More than 100 cases in that period involved at least 10 invoices being sent to the department concerning individual clients whose charges were dealt with together in or in quick succession before the District Court.

stories.title: Islanders asked to 'track' not 'trample' Asian hornets
stories.summary: More than 200 Asian hornet queens have been caught and nine nests destroyed so far this year. People in Guernsey are being asked to "track" not "trample" Asian hornets in an effort to protect public health and the island's biodiversity. Asian hornets spread through Europe after arriving in southern France in 2004, having reportedly hitched a ride in a consignment of pottery from Asia. They first appeared in Guernsey in 2017 and the States said the non-native, invasive species poses a major threat to the island's biodiversity and honeybees, which make up 39% of its diet on average.

stories.title: Google hit with $1 billion fine for breaking EU antitrust rules
stories.summary: The European Union has fined Google’s parent company Alphabet €890 million (about $1 billion) for two separate violations of the bloc’s Digital Markets Act (DMA). One penalty is for giving its own products preferential treatment in search results, while the other is for blocking Android developers from sending users to alternate payment options. A €460 million fine has been issued to Google for giving preferential treatment to its own Shopping, Hotels, and Flights services in Google Search results. The second €430 million penalty is for Play Store rules preventing developers from freely steering consumers to alternative payment systems that may be cheaper.

stories.title: 'Life-changing' MS drug to help people walk now on NHS in England
stories.summary: Fampridine boosts nerve signals to make it easier to walk, with up to 5,000 eligible. The first drug to help people with multiple sclerosis walk is being made available on the NHS in England. Fampridine boosts nerve signals to make it easier for some people to move and live independently. Aysen Slack, who used to pay for fampridine privately until it became too costly, said the decision would make a "huge difference". Up to 5,000 people in England could benefit each year. The drug is already available elsewhere in the UK. Multiple sclerosis is caused by the immune system attacking the protective covering around nerve cells - known as myelin.

offbeat.title: Rosalía pide perdón tras la tormenta digital en Argentina por compartir un vídeo de la victoria de España
offbeat.summary: La cantante Rosalía se ha visto obligada a disculparse tras compartir en sus redes sociales un post que ha generado polémica en Argentina. La cantante catalana reposteó un vídeo de la antigua actriz de cine para adultos Mia Khalifa, en la que la ahora influencer celebraba el triunfo de España desde un balcón en Nueva York. De fondo en esa grabación sonaba La Perla, una de las canciones más famosas del disco LUX de Rosalía, mientras un texto sobreimpreso proclamaba que la vida sonaba distinta ahora que “las Perlas” (en referencia al equipo argentino) habían sido derrotadas.

offbeat.title: Silvia Intxaurrondo y RTVE buscan fórmulas para que la presentadora siga en la cadena incluso si pierde su demanda
offbeat.summary: Silvia Intxaurrondo y los directivos de Radio Televisión Española (RTVE) buscan fórmulas para que la presentadora siga en la cadena pública incluso si pierde su demanda laboral contra la corporación. Según ha podido confirmar EL ESPAÑOL-Invertia con fuentes conocedoras, la principal alternativa podría ser que la periodista renuncie a su contrato como personal fijo de RTVE para que luego selle una nueva vinculación como personal externo. Estas mismas fuentes indican que hay una clara voluntad de las partes para que la profesional siga en la pública presentando La hora de La 1 con lo que se alejan los fantasmas de una posible salida de la cadena.

offbeat.title: Un automobiliste de 18 ans mis en examen à Marseille pour tentative de meurtre sur un policier après un refus d'obtempérer
offbeat.summary: Un homme de 18 ans a été mis en examen et placé en détention provisoire pour tentative de meurtre sur une personne dépositaire de l'autorité publique après avoir grièvement blessé un policier lors d'un refus d'obtempérer, lundi à Marseille, indique jeudi 23 juillet le procureur de la République de Marseille, Nicolas Bessone. Trois mineurs, âgés de 13 et 16 ans, qui se trouvaient à bord du véhicule volé, ont également été mis en examen pour des faits liés à cette affaire.

offbeat.title: Wreckage of Pan Am plane that shaped aviation safety found 74 years on
offbeat.summary: 21 hours ago Zahra Fatima The wreckage of a Pan American Airways plane has been found 74 years after it plunged into the Atlantic Ocean in a crash that prompted mandatory airline safety briefings. The Clipper Endeavor was found 2,000ft (610m) below sea level off the coast of Puerto Rico with a sonar-equipped drone. It went down on 11 April 1952 following multiple-engine failure shortly after take-off. Everyone onboard survived the impact - but passengers struggled to locate life vests and rafts as the plane rapidly sank. Of the 69 passengers and crew onboard, just 17 survived.

offbeat.title: Meurtre de Thomas à Crépol : la circonstance aggravante de «racisme» finalement retenue par les juges
offbeat.summary: C’est un rebondissement majeur dans ce dossier. Les magistrats en charge de l’enquête sur le meurtre de Thomas, à Crépol, le 18 novembre 2023, ont informé les parties de la mise en examen supplétive des 14 mis en cause pour meurtre et tentative de meurtre avec la circonstance aggravante de «racisme», a appris Le Figaro de sources concordantes. La nouvelle intervient alors que l’enquête vient d’être clôturée, et qu’un procès se profilait pour les mis en examen l’année prochaine. Selon nos informations, cette étape survient après de nouveaux interrogatoires des mis en cause, qui se sont déroulés en début d’été, et qui ont abouti à cette mise en examen.

offbeat.summary: Arsenal have reached a €40million (£34m) agreement with Club Brugge for the signing of winger Christos Tzolis. The north London club are in the process of finalising a move for the 24-year-old. The Athletic reported on June 12 that the Premier League club were exploring a deal for the Tzolis, with sources saying that they believed a deal could be possible for around €40m. Arsenal have been looking to strengthen their attack this summer, with the pursuit of Tzolis separate from any interest in signing Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa or a move for any other target. The departure of versatile forward Leandro Trossard to Besiktas in a €20m deal was confirmed this week.

offbeat.title: Festival Kwaku stopt in huidige vorm, organisatie overlegt met gemeente over alternatief
offbeat.summary: Het Amsterdamse Kwaku Summer Festival stopt volgend jaar in de huidige vorm. De organisatie noemt als reden de stijgende kosten, aangescherpte vergunningsvoorwaarden van de gemeente en toegenomen organisatorische complexiteit. Voor de organisatie was het geen makkelijk besluit. "Kwaku is meer dan een festival - het is een uniek maatschappelijk fenomeen dat vanouds voor heel veel mensen veel betekent", staat in een persbericht. Het is nog niet duidelijk of het multiculturele festival helemaal verdwijnt of na de editie van deze zomer toch nog terugkeert in een andere vorm. De organisatie meldt hierover in overleg te zijn met het stadsdeel Zuidoost.

offbeat.title: Einspruch des TSV im Toto-Pokal: »Er war nicht spielberechtigt«
offbeat.summary: Die 1:3-Niederlage in der 1. Runde des Toto-Pokals des TSV Neudrossenfeld gegen Eintracht Bamberg hat ein Nachspiel. Nach dem Abpfiff hat der Bayernligist Einspruch gegen die Wertung der Partie eingelegt. Nun liegt der Ball beim Bayerischen Fußball-Verband (BFV), der am grünen Tisch über den Ausgang entscheiden muss. Was war passiert? Bis zur 71. Minute beim Stand von 1:2 lief die Begegnung in geordneten Bahnen. Dann wechselten die Bamberger erneut – und der neue Mann schlug augenblicklich ein. In der 75. Minute erzielte der Eingewechselte das vermeintliche 3:1 für den FCE. Doch die Freude währte nur kurz, denn plötzlich unterbrach das Schiedsrichtergespann die Partie.

offbeat.title: UniCredit steht kurz vor Commerzbank-Übernahme
offbeat.summary: Die italienische Großbank UniCredit steht kurz vor der Übernahme der Commerzbank. Bankchef Orcel sucht dazu jetzt das Gespräch mit allen Beteiligten. Willkommen ist er allerdings nicht. Geht es nach UniCredit-Chef Andrea Orcel, ist die Übernahme der Commerzbank bereits beschlossene Sache. "Die Commerzbank entwickelt sich von einer attraktiven Finanzbeteiligung zu einer strategischen Transaktion mit substanziellem industriellen Wert durch die Umsetzung unserer bewährten Blaupause", heißt es in einer Präsentation von Orcel zu den Quartalszahlen. Mit den Genehmigungen der Aufseher sei vielleicht noch in diesem Jahr zu rechnen.

offbeat.title: Maxence Lacroix to Chelsea: Crystal Palace defender is quick, excels when one-on-one and is a leader now too
offbeat.summary:
Chelsea have not paid a transfer fee for a player aged 26 or over since the summer of 2022 with the ill-fated acquisitions of Raheem Sterling and Kalidou Koulibaly. But Crystal Palace’s Maxence Lacroix is a player worth relaxing the policy for.

Far from a veteran, Lacroix, 26, is entering his prime after two strong seasons with Palace. Beyond Selhurst Park, perhaps the Frenchman has gone a little under the radar. But, particularly since Marc Guehi's exit, there is growing awareness of his key role.

Oliver Glasner, who worked with him in both Germany and England, rather summed it up with his own verdict on the player recently.

offbeat.summary: Stadtteile Uellendahl - Dönberg Uellendahl Betrüger während der Übergabe festgenommen Wuppertal · Die Polizei hat am Dienstag (21. Juli 2026) einen mutmaßlichen Trickbetrüger im Bereich Uellendahl festgenommen. Nach einem Komplizen wird noch gefahndet. 23.07.2026 , 13:22 Uhr Symbolbild. Foto: Christoph Petersen Ein Ehepaar war am 17. Juli durch Unbekannte, die vorgaben, Bankmitarbeiter und Polizisten zu sein, um einen fünfstelligen Euro-Betrag betrogen worden. „Neben der telefonischen Kontaktaufnahme kommunizierten die Betrüger über einen Messenger-Dienst. Die Senioren übergaben einem falschen Polizeibeamten das Geld an ihrer Wohnanschrift“, heißt es aus dem Präsidium.

offbeat.title: I wanted a clock that never needed setting. Things escalated.
offbeat.summary: Sure, maybe your clock has a backup battery, but mine has a deployment pipeline! Let’s be clock bros Sure, maybe your clock has a backup battery, but mine has a deployment pipeline! Rapid prototyping! Credit: Lee Hutchinson Rapid prototyping! Credit: Lee Hutchinson I wanted a clock that, annoyingly, didn’t seem to exist. Since childhood, my bedside clocks have been a series of red, seven-segment LED clock-radio specials from Walmart or Target. They are invariably cheap, simple, and long-lived—but they require manual intervention at the start and end of Daylight Saving Time and whenever the power flickers.

offbeat.title: Scheffler not keen on McIlroy-DeChambeau feud
offbeat.summary: Scottie Scheffler not keen on Rory McIlroy and Bryson DeChambeau feud Updated / Thursday, 23 Jul 2026 10:38 Getty World number one Scottie Scheffler is playing in the 3M Open in Minnesota, starting today World number one Scottie Scheffler admits he has "never been a big fan of personal jabs" after Rory McIlroy publicly criticised Bryson DeChambeau at last week's Open Championship.

offbeat.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
offbeat.summary: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region. About 12,000 people have been evacuated from the south-west of France and authorities are moving 8,800 others as wildfires spread in the Gironde region, the local prefect has said. Sophie Brocas told reporters 2,400 hectares (5,900 acres) of land had already been destroyed around the Bay of Arcachon. Many of those who had to leave the area overnight were visitors to local campsites on the Lège-Cap-Ferret peninsula and the latest evacuations also involved campsites as a precaution. Wildfires have also broken out in Spain and Italy in recent days.

offbeat.title: LIVE: Kijk hier naar het skûtsjesilen bij Langweer
offbeat.summary: Kijk hier naar de vijfde wedstrijd van het SKS-kampioenschap skûtsjesilen 2026. Vandaag is de wedstrijd op de Langwarder Wielen. Kan Langweer stunten op het thuiswater? En welk skûtsje grijpt de macht in het algemeen klassement? De wedstrijd begint om 14.00 uur met een tien-minutenschot. Het startschot is om 14.10 uur. Het skûtsjesilen is iedere dag live te zien bij Omrop Fryslân op televisie. Daar stopt de uitzending als alle skûtsjes over de finish zijn gekomen. Online gaan we langer door met reacties van schippers. Omrop Fryslân is ook via WhatsApp te volgen. Via ons WhatsApp-kanaal sturen we je interessante berichten en video’s uit Fryslân en blijf je op de hoogte. Abonneer je hier.

offbeat.title: Zimbabwe look to expose vulnerable India searching for winning formula
offbeat.summary: Big picture: Shreyas Iyer looking for first win India 's role in helping smaller cricket economies generate income via TV rights generally dominates chatter ahead of a Zimbabwe tour. But series defeats to Ireland and England have veered the discussion towards India's T20I failings and team combinations. When they arrived in Zimbabwe two years ago, they were led by Shubman Gill. Now, T20Is are the only format Gill isn't part of. The captaincy mantle has been passed on from Suryakumar Yadav to Shreyas Iyer , who is winless as captain after seven games.

offbeat.title: Football gossip: Barcola, Scott, Fernandes, Odegaard, Willock, Camavinga, Disasi
offbeat.summary: Liverpool are confident of signing Bradley Barcola, Alex Scott favours a move to Arsenal , Bruno Fernandes is ready to commit his future to Manchester United . Liverpool are confident they can beat Arsenal and Bayern Munich to the signature of Paris St-Germain forward Bradley Barcola, after the 23-year-old impressed with four goal contributions at the World Cup with France this summer. (Sun) , external Arsenal are edging the battle with Manchester United for sought-after Bournemouth midfielder Alex Scott, 22 - but the England Under-21 international has concerns around how much game time he would get at the Emirates.

offbeat.title: ¿Puedo dejar la basura en el rellano? La Ley de Propiedad Horizontal lo aclara
offbeat.summary: La convivencia entre vecinos no siempre es sencilla. Compartir espacios comunes implica respetar unas normas básicas para garantizar el bienestar de todos los residentes, pero no siempre ocurre así. Los ruidos fuera del horario permitido, la suciedad en las zonas comunes y el uso inadecuado del ascensor o discusiones por pequeños conflictos forman parte del día a día en muchas comunidades de propietarios.

offbeat.title: Schipper Joure: "We hebben echt enorm staan schreeuwen naar het publiek"
offbeat.summary: Schipper Rinus de Jong uit Joure is bekomen van de aanvaring van gisteren. Hij belandde met zijn skûtsje in het publiek, toen het eerste schip al over de finish was gekomen in de Lange Sleat. De Jong zegt dat de SKS moet praten over het veranderen van de regels, zodat zoiets volgend jaar niet weer kan gebeuren. "Het was hartstikke spannend, maar gelukkig is het allemaal goed afgelopen. We hebben niet veel schade: een paar krassen op het zwaard en een deukje in de kop, dus dat valt reuze mee, maar het was wel levensgevaarlijk wat er gebeurde.

offbeat.title: Politica Daniëlle T. uit Stadskanaal veroordeeld voor stalken van radiopresentator
offbeat.summary: Ze hadden een affaire, radiopresentator Fernando Halman en politica Daniëlle T. uit Stadskanaal. Na die affaire, die hij beëindigde, kreeg Halman in 2023 honderden telefoontjes en berichtjes van haar. Hij deed aangifte en T. werd woensdag veroordeeld tot een taakstraf en een voorwaardelijke celstraf. De rechtbank vindt het bewezen dat de vrouw stelselmatig inbreuk heeft gemaakt op de levenssfeer van de presentator van NPO's jongerenzender FunX. Ze ziet, zo schrijft de rechtbank, zichzelf als slachtoffer en ontkent dat ze iets strafbaars gedaan heeft. Daarom legt de rechtbank haar naast een taakstraf van 120 uur en een voorwaardelijke celstraf van drie maanden ook een contactverbod op.

offbeat.title: White House report says Trump can usher in a "new golden age" of science
offbeat.summary: The resulting report is a near-random mixture of real problems and grievances. Gold-plated? It's a golden age, of sorts, in the White House at least. Credit: Bloomberg On Tuesday, the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) released a report entitled “Science: A New Golden Age,” in which it lays out how it has viewed science, found it lacking, and believes the Trump administration is in the perfect position to fix things. It’s a bit unexpected coming from an administration that has been proposing crippling funding cuts to research and trying to enable political appointees to terminate grants awarded based on scientific merit.

offbeat.title: Day 6 morning training report
offbeat.summary: 22/07/2026 The Azzurri got involved in a morning session at the SKI.IT Arena in Dimaro on the day of their first pre-season friendly against Arezzo. The squad completed a final training session which involved a series of set-piece drills. Billy Gilmour has begun a rehabilitation programme.

offbeat.title: NFL Power Rankings, 2026: Ranking the best defense in every division
offbeat.summary: 6. NFC North: Green Bay Packers There is obviously some concern over the Green Bay Packers' defense, at least at the start of the season, due to the ongoing recovery process of superstar pass rusher Micah Parsons. When Parsons went down late last season against the Broncos with a knee injury, the Packers' entire season went off the rails. Now, they are going to be getting used to life without guys like Rashan Gary (who may have worn out his welcome anyway), Quay Walker, and even depth pieces like Kingsley Enagbare. The new defensive coordinator is former Cardinals head coach Jonathan Gannon, who is taking over with Jeff Hafley off to the Miami Dolphins as their new head coach.

offbeat.title: Department of Justice received 78 legal aid invoices for individual’s theft charges
offbeat.summary: Solicitors secured 78 criminal legal aid certificates for representing one accused charged with theft offences before the District Court last year. The Dublin case, involving at least as many charges of theft, was one of more than 30 in the last 18 months to accrue dozens of legal aid certificates, which are essentially payment invoices, according to data from the Department of Justice . More than 100 cases in that period involved at least 10 invoices being sent to the department concerning individual clients whose charges were dealt with together in or in quick succession before the District Court.

breaking.title: 'I am still alive': Indian activist on hunger strike for 26 days loses 11kg
breaking.summary: Sonam Wangchuk has been the main draw at the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protest in Delhi. The violence has stirred public anger and galvanised India's opposition, posing a challenge for Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government. Modi has not commented directly on the protests or engaged with the protesters, but on Thursday, he posted a statement on X that is being seen as an attempt to reach out to students who have been frustrated with repeated exam paper leaks . "Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth," he wrote. "We have decided to set up fast-track courts to ensure swift and stringent punishment for those involved in paper leaks...

breaking.title: Zelensky’s Dizzying U-Turn Is the Latest Zigzag in a Presidency Full of Them
breaking.summary: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine has pivoted from unpopular moves before, but a recent decision about military leadership and strategy could weaken his position.

breaking.title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
breaking.summary: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.

crisis.title: Business rate cut not enough, says Michelin-star chef
crisis.summary: Alex Claridge, owner of The Wilderness in Birmingham, said the proposal did not go far enough. For Michelin-starred chef Alex Claridge, running an independent restaurant has become a difficult task. Despite the acclaim his Birmingham restaurant The Wilderness commands, Claridge says the reality behind the kitchen doors is one of soaring costs, shrinking margins and constant uncertainty. It's a picture he believes is shared by independent hospitality businesses across the country.

crisis.title: Global Oil Price Nears $100 a Barrel as Conflict With Iran Deepens
crisis.summary: The Houthis claimed to have attacked two ships in the Red Sea, and Iran said a third caught fire near the Strait of Hormuz.

entertainment.title: Ex-Coronation Street actor to receive 'substantial' libel damages from the Sun publisher
entertainment.summary: Qasim Akhtar is to receive "substantial" damages to settle a High Court libel claim from a story last eyar. Former Coronation Street actor Qasim Akhtar is to receive "substantial" damages from the Sun's publisher, to settle a High Court libel claim. Akhtar who played Zeedan Nazir in the ITV soap from 2014 to 2018 and then 2021 to 2023, sued News Group Newspapers (NGN) earlier this year. A story in the Sun had falsely alleged Akhtar had "backed" and "teamed up with" Islamic cleric Uthman ibn Farooq, was associated with extremism, radicalisation and violence, and had "moved to Pakistan" and "trained with guns".

facts.title: Illegal waste investigation sees £275k forfeited
facts.summary: The investigation spans Herefordshire, Worcestershire and Shropshire, police say. Three businesses that are being investigated by police who are looking into illegal waste activity have forfeited a total of more than £275,000. West Mercia Police said additional crimes had been identified in its investigation across Herefordshire, Worcestershire and Shropshire. The force added that more assets were under review and a money laundering investigation was still active. It comes after two men were arrested at an address in Drakes Broughton, Worcestershire, in January. Six firearms were seized at that location, along with about £18,000 in cash.

facts.title: Islanders asked to 'track' not 'trample' Asian hornets
facts.summary: More than 200 Asian hornet queens have been caught and nine nests destroyed so far this year. People in Guernsey are being asked to "track" not "trample" Asian hornets in an effort to protect public health and the island's biodiversity. Asian hornets spread through Europe after arriving in southern France in 2004, having reportedly hitched a ride in a consignment of pottery from Asia. They first appeared in Guernsey in 2017 and the States said the non-native, invasive species poses a major threat to the island's biodiversity and honeybees, which make up 39% of its diet on average.

sports.title: Richards hopeful of completing medal set in Glasgow
sports.summary: Double Olympic champion Matt Richards is hoping to claim Commonwealth gold and complete his major medal collection. The 23-year-old swimmer won Olympic golds in 2021 and 2024 and has multiple world and European titles to his name. But four fourth-place finishes in Birmingham in 2022 has left a hole in an otherwise glittering trophy cabinet. "It would mean the world," said Richards, of winning gold in Glasgow 2026. "Every time I've ever stood at the top of the podium I've heard the British national anthem, which is fantastic and amazing and every time it's special. "But I've never been able to stand at the top of the podium and hear the Welsh anthem and that's something important to me.

sports.title: England U16 player fight was resolved quickly - FA
sports.summary: The FA says a fight between two players at an England Under-16s camp was caused by an "internal matter". A fight between England Under-16 players at a camp in Turkey was caused by an "internal matter" which was "quickly resolved", says the FA. An online clip from the camp in late-2025 shows two players fighting in a hotel room, while several others film and encourage them. The FA said the issue has been settled and no further action will be taken. An FA spokesperson said: "We were made aware of an internal matter during an Under-16s camp in October 2025.

sports.title: Johnson-Thompson and Asher-Smith out of Commonwealths
sports.summary: Olympic silver medallist Katarina Johnson-Thompson pulls out of the Commonwealth Games because of injury. Olympic silver medallist Katarina Johnson-Thompson has pulled out of the Commonwealth Games because of injury. Dina Asher-Smith and 400m Olympic silver medalist Matt Hudson-Smith, who were both set to run relays for Team England in Glasgow, have also been replaced, as has Asher-Smith's sprint team-mate Daryll Neita and 400m runner Amber Anning. Johnson-Thompson, 33, was due to compete in her third Commonwealth Games heptathlon, having won gold on the Gold Coast in 2018 and at Birmingham in 2022.

politics.title: Poorest children in England 40% more likely to be hospitalised for lung infections
politics.summary: Children living in the poorest areas across England are 40% more likely to be admitted to hospital for respiratory infections, an analysis of NHS England data shows, with experts raising the alarm on the impact of air pollution on young people’s health. The analysis, based on data obtained via freedom of information requests by the charity Impact on Urban Health, revealed 1,941 out of every 100,000 children living in the most deprived fifth of England were admitted to hospital for respiratory illnesses in 2024-25.

politics.title: Gary Lineker among millionaires asking Andy Burnham to tax them more
politics.summary: A group including the former England footballer write to the new PM telling him "we can afford it". Millionaires including former footballer Gary Lineker and music producer Brian Eno have written to new prime minister Andy Burnham asking to be taxed more. In an open letter, 120 well-off Britons told Burnham: "We can afford it. We're not talking about higher taxes on those who get up and go to work for their income every day, but on the very richest whose income is derived from the wealth they hold." Organised by Patriotic Millionaires, the letter said it would lead to a more equal society, and urged a "devolution of wealth and power from the very richest".

politics.title: Nuclear Energy Revival Puts Westinghouse in Prime Position
politics.summary: The company, which filed for bankruptcy protection in 2017, stands to benefit from growing support for nuclear power and President Trump’s deal with Saudi Arabia.

health.title: ‘Why do I feel crappy?’: women are learning about their luteal phases - and Maha is weaponizing it
health.summary: E mily Rae Detrick, a health coach, wants women to be more in touch with their menstrual cycle in its entirety. This, she said on an October episode of Culture Apothecary with Alex Clark, a podcast popular among “Make America Healthy Again” (Maha) adherents, is “a way that you honor God and the way we were created”. It also serves her self-assigned mission to help “make America fertile again”. In the episode and on social media, Detrick speaks at length about the luteal phase, the 14-ish days between ovulation and menstruation when women are more likely to experience irritability, bloating and food cravings.

health.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
health.summary: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.

health.title: 'Life-changing' MS drug to help people walk now on NHS in England
health.summary: Fampridine boosts nerve signals to make it easier to walk, with up to 5,000 eligible. The first drug to help people with multiple sclerosis walk is being made available on the NHS in England. Fampridine boosts nerve signals to make it easier for some people to move and live independently. Aysen Slack, who used to pay for fampridine privately until it became too costly, said the decision would make a "huge difference". Up to 5,000 people in England could benefit each year. The drug is already available elsewhere in the UK. Multiple sclerosis is caused by the immune system attacking the protective covering around nerve cells - known as myelin.

tech.title: The right-wing boomers protesting data centers have a lot in common with the left
tech.summary: On a gray, humid Saturday morning in central Florida, a little under a dozen people gathered outside the Spring Hill Branch Library to protest the construction of a hyperscale data center in their community. There was no immediate threat — the Hernando County commission had unanimously approved a one-year moratorium on such developments in June — but the organizers weren’t satisfied. A temporary pause wouldn’t be enough. They wanted a ban. “We are going to fight, fight, fight,” said a 71-year-old woman named Carol, who was manning a table with flyers and a petition the group, No Data Centers Hernando County, planned on presenting to the local government.

tech.title: Google hit with $1 billion fine for breaking EU antitrust rules
tech.summary: The European Union has fined Google’s parent company Alphabet €890 million (about $1 billion) for two separate violations of the bloc’s Digital Markets Act (DMA). One penalty is for giving its own products preferential treatment in search results, while the other is for blocking Android developers from sending users to alternate payment options. A €460 million fine has been issued to Google for giving preferential treatment to its own Shopping, Hotels, and Flights services in Google Search results. The second €430 million penalty is for Play Store rules preventing developers from freely steering consumers to alternative payment systems that may be cheaper.
